forbidden_actions: Edit, stage, commit, merge, rebase, cherry-pick, revert, apply, or push any file under /home/matheus/ARIS/Project_ARIS during the future materialization phase.
Run any Project_ARIS unit test, integration test, CI rerun, harness, runtime launcher, or focused test as part of the future materialization phase.
Execute RED, reset, GREEN, benign flow, rollback, or kill-switch during the future materialization phase.
Create any artifact that states or implies a new proof-loop execution occurred in the future materialization phase.
Open PURG05 or mutate ACTIVE_CONTEXT_STATE.json to point at PURG05 in the future materialization phase.
Declare the finding closed, resolved, safe, product ready, or Bedrock ready from the future materialization phase.
Invert runtime, real apply, secrets, dependency, product, Bedrock, MCP, RAG, memory-write, or external-network locks.
Use package managers, dependency installation, or any command whose only purpose is to recreate missing proof evidence by fresh execution.
Invent missing evidence that is not derivable from the verified source artifacts and hashes recorded in the readiness packet.
forbidden_claims: The future materialization phase must not say a new proof loop ran.
The future materialization phase must not say Project_ARIS was touched.
The future materialization phase must not say PURG05 can open now.
The future materialization phase must not say remediation became proven.
The future materialization phase must not say the finding is closed.
